ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn code exists for Thai Style Spicy and Sour Lala; FOODON_00003009 (seafood dish) is the closest parent category available.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No specific FoodEx2 code exists for a prepared Thai-style spicy sour clam dish; A0CKP (Clams and cockles, bivalve molluscs) from the EFSA FoodEx2 system is the closest parent code based on the primary ingredient.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for this prepared dish; 22746000 (Clam) is the closest available code representing the primary ingredient.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Thai Style Spicy & Sour Lala, often shared or consumed as a main dish, is approximately 250 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Lala Goreng Bercili (Stir-fried Clams with Chili)
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia, Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are based on 'Lala Goreng Bercili' from the Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD 2017), which is a direct match for stir-fried spicy clams, consistent with the primary ingredient and preparation method of Thai Style Spicy & Sour Lala.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate for low-carbohydrate seafood dish)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index estimated from similar low-carbohydrate seafood dishes (e.g., fish, prawns, bivalves) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Although the dish contains some sugar and tamarind, the overall carbohydrate content is low at 7g per 100g, resulting in an effectively negligible GI. GI set to 1 (lowest valid positive value) per Veritas rules for low-carbohydrate protein-dominant dishes; gi_category set to low.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and seasoning sauces.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish in moderation due to its elevated sodium content of approximately 450 mg per 100g, and may request reduced-sodium preparation or limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index, with moderate added sugar from sauce.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions, as its low glycemic index and high protein content from clams support stable postprandial blood glucose, though portion control is advised due to added sugars in the sauce.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat content and lean protein from clams, with sodium as a secondary concern.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish can be a reasonable choice for individuals managing cardiovascular disease, as clams provide lean protein and beneficial micronutrients, but sodium intake should be monitored and portion size controlled.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Thai Style Spicy & Sour Lala is a popular Malaysian Zi Char (stir-fry) dish featuring fresh clams (lala) stir-fried with a vibrant, aromatic, and intensely flavored sauce. The 'Thai Style' refers to the characteristic balance of spicy, sour, and savory notes, typically achieved with ingredients such as chili padi, garlic, shallots, tamarind paste, lime juice, and fish sauce, often with a hint of sweetness from added sugar. The clams are quickly cooked until just tender, retaining their natural brininess and delicate texture while absorbing the bold flavors of the sauce. It is commonly garnished with fresh herbs like coriander or spring onions. Nutritionally, it provides approximately 108 kcal per 100g, with 10.7g of lean protein from the clams, 4g of fat, and 7g of carbohydrates. The dish has a low glycemic index, making it suitable for blood sugar management. However, it can be high in sodium (approximately 450 mg per 100g) due to the fish sauce and seasoning used, which is a consideration for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. It is a good source of iron, calcium, and vitamin A from the clams and vegetables.
common_user_queries: Recipe for Thai style spicy clams

confidence_reason: High confidence based on direct nutrient match from IMR Malaysia MyFCD for 'Lala Goreng Bercili', which is highly analogous to Thai Style Spicy & Sour Lala in primary ingredient and preparation method. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable low-carbohydrate seafood dishes.
